task.title: Purge generated thumbnails older than 30 days without deleting originals
task.description: The image pipeline generates thumbnails under `storage/thumbnails/` while originals live under `storage/uploads/`. Thumbnails can be regenerated, but originals must never be deleted. Implement a purge job that deletes thumbnails older than 30 days, and keep a restore mechanism (no-op if directory missing).
constraints: Do NOT delete any files under storage/uploads/.
Perform a sample verification (count + spot-check) before deletion.
Keep a 'trash' staging move for 24h before permanent delete.
success_criteria: Only thumbnails older than 30 days are removed.

steps.thought: Read task requirements and confirm which directories hold originals vs thumbnails.
steps.observation: Task: purge storage/thumbnails older than 30 days; never touch storage/uploads; use trash staging.

steps.thought: Inspect storage directories and approximate sizes.
Confirm thumbnails structure (hashed subdirs) and file types.
Create a trash directory under storage/thumbnails/.trash (same filesystem).
List candidate thumbnails older than 30 days (no deletion yet).
Count candidates to estimate impact and record baseline.
Spot-check that candidate paths do not include storage/uploads.

arguments.command: cd /workspace && find -P storage/thumbnails -type f -mtime +30 | head -n 200 | rg -n "storage/uploads" || echo OK

steps.thought: Implement purge script that first moves files to .trash, preserving relative paths.

arguments.new_str:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
BASE="storage/thumbnails"
TRASH="$BASE/.trash"
CUTOFF_DAYS=30
MODE=${1:-dry-run}

mkdir -p "$TRASH"
mapfile -t FILES < <(find -P "$BASE" -type f -mtime +"$CUTOFF_DAYS" -print)

echo "Found ${#FILES[@]} thumbnails older than $CUTOFF_DAYS days"
if [[ "$MODE" == "dry-run" ]]; then
  printf '%s\n' "${FILES[@]}" | head -n 50
  exit 0
fi

for f in "${FILES[@]}"; do
  if [[ "$f" != "$BASE"/* ]]; then
    echo "Refuse outside base: $f" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
  rel=${f#"$BASE"/}
  mkdir -p "$TRASH/$(dirname "$rel")"
  mv -- "$f" "$TRASH/$rel"
done

echo "Moved ${#FILES[@]} files to trash"

steps.thought: Run dry-run of purge script to confirm it only targets thumbnails.
steps.observation:
Found 284321 thumbnails older than 30 days
storage/thumbnails/...
...

steps.thought: Apply purge (move to trash) and capture completion count.
Verify originals count hasn't changed (quickly sample).
Verify thumbnails directory size decreased and trash increased (expected).
Add a second script to permanently delete trash older than 1 day (24h quarantine).

arguments.new_str:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
TRASH="storage/thumbnails/.trash"
CUTOFF_HOURS=24
find -P "$TRASH" -type f -mmin +$((CUTOFF_HOURS*60)) -delete
# remove empty dirs
find -P "$TRASH" -type d -empty -delete
echo "Trash cleanup complete"

steps.thought: Document the two-phase retention workflow in ops docs.

arguments.new_str:
Two-phase thumbnail retention:
1) Nightly: `scripts/purge_thumbnails.sh apply` moves thumbnails older than 30 days into `storage/thumbnails/.trash`.
2) Nightly+1: `scripts/empty_thumbnail_trash.sh` deletes trash files older than 24h.
Guards: find -P, prefix checks; NEVER touches storage/uploads.

steps.thought: Run a small integration test: generate a new thumbnail and ensure purge doesn't delete it.

arguments.command: cd /workspace && python scripts/generate_test_thumbnail.py && bash scripts/purge_thumbnails.sh dry-run | rg -n "test_thumbnail" || echo OK

steps.thought: Submit summary with counts and explicit statement originals unaffected.

arguments.message: Implemented two-phase thumbnail retention: purge script moves thumbnails older than 30 days from storage/thumbnails into storage/thumbnails/.trash (24h quarantine) with prefix guard + find -P (no symlink follow). Verified 284,321 candidates, applied move, checked storage/uploads file count unchanged, and documented workflow in docs/ops/thumbnail_retention.md. Added empty_thumbnail_trash.sh to delete trash after 24h.
